characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Badass Family: As each member of their family comes into and discovers their power, it becomes clear that the Kinomoto family (and their friends and associates) is a close knit group of powerful magic users.Best shown in the climax of the Clear Cloud Arc, where Sakura's family and friends band together to help her. It's why Kaito choose to make Akiho, Sakura's twin. If Akiho's real family comes after her, the Kinomotos can protect her. Family Theme Naming: Combined withFloral Theme Naming. All members of the Kinomoto family have given names of flowers. Sakura is Japanese forCherry Blossoms, Touya's name contains the kanji for "peach blossom", the "fuji" in Fujitaka means "wisteria" and Nadeshiko is a pink variety of dianthus (more specifically,Dianthus superbus, which is held as a symbol ofthe ideal feminine archetypein Japan). Open Secret:Everyone in the family knew that Sakura was involved in something mysterious and magical since the fourth grade, if they didn't want to pry in to the details. It helps that everyone in the family is magical and that the main characters weren't particularly subtle.

characters.name: Touya Kinomoto
characters.description: Sakura's big brother. He often teases her, calling her "monster" but he has a hugeBig Brother Instincttoward her. He is best friends with Yukito, and has a tendency to switch jobs a lot. Touya can see ghosts, including his mother's when she visits. The Ace: He's very talented, and can do pretty much anything— he cooks, cleans, sews, plays soccer, violin, piano, and organ, is a black belt in karate, a good student, and works every job under the sun, everything from waiting tables to working as a costumed stuntman for kids'TVshows. Beta Couple: He and Yukito are this to Syaoran and Sakura. Like Syaoran, he has a hard time actually getting the words out to Yukito, but unlike Syaoran, he's already been in love with Yukito for a while, and is very comfortable with and casual about his closeness to Yukito before actually making his feelings known. Big Brother Bully: He likes teasing Sakura by calling her "monster", and relentlessly makes fun of many of her other various flaws and mistakes. Even though he doesn’t truly mean it, he does enjoy provoking a reaction out of her. Big Brother Instinct: Abusing his sister within his hearing/watching range will not end well for the abuser. The only thing that gives him pause before giving his magic to Yukito/Yue is that he'll no longer know when Sakura is in danger, and he makes Yue promise to protect her in his stead. In the Clear Card arc, he becomes concerned about Sakura againafter gaining his magic back.

characters.name: Fujitaka Kinomoto
characters.description: Sakura and Touya's father. A kindhearted middle-class man, Fujitaka married Nadeshiko when she was just sixteen when he worked as a teacher. He later became a university professor in archaeology. Age-Gap Romance: With his wife Nadeshiko. Nadeshiko was a sixteen-year-old schoolgirl and him a teacher who was starting to teach when they got married. Dub Name Change: In the English dub, he becomes Aiden Avalon. Fourth-Date Marriage: Fujitaka and Nadeshiko got married very early on in their relationship. Happily Married: He treasures the time he spent with Nadeshiko before her death and honors the promise he made to her that he wouldn't cry over her death.

characters.name: Nadeshiko Kinomoto, nèe Amamiya
characters.description: Sakura's deceased mother who was a famous model. Nadeshiko was Sonomi's best friend and cousin whom Sonomi wasveryprotective of. Adaptation Dye-Job: In the manga, Nadeshiko's hair is black with a purple hue. In the anime, her hair appears brown with a grey hue. Age-Gap Romance: With her husband Fujitaka. She was a sixteen-year-old schoolgirl and Fujitaka a teacher starting out his career when they got married. Angelic Beauty: Evenbeforeshe died, Nadeshiko was such a beauty that her husband compared her to an angel. In the series, she appears as an actual angel and looking just as beautiful as when she was alive. Athletically Challenged: In a sharp contrast to her daughter, Nadeshiko wasterribleat sports. When she went skiing, she slammed into a tree. When she tried to jump over a vaulting box in PE class, she slammedintoit instead, scattering its sections across the floor.

characters.name: Yukito Tsukishiro
characters.description: Touya's best friend and Sakura's first love interest. A kind, bespectacledBig Eater, Yukito is incredibly friendly with Sakura and her friends. The Ace: Ridiculously gifted in athletics, very surprising considering he wears glasses. Case in point: he can wipe the floor with the basketball team multiple timesby himself. It's implied that every sport at his school wants him for their team, but aside from occasionally allowing himself to be recruited as a stand-in for absent players, he refuses to commit to any of them. The only sport he appears to have a genuine interest in is archery. He enters and wins a competition during the series. Not surprising, given that as Yue his weapon of choice is a supply of beam arrows. Alternate Identity Amnesia: Yukito can't recall what his Yue side does. While Yukito later becomes aware of his 'other self', Yue admits he has yet to allow Yukito to see through his eyes.Yukito takes steps to avert this inClear Card,using the power of the Tsukimine shrine to give himself magic that includes remembering what Yue sees. Beta Couple: He and Touya are to Syaoran and Sakura. In the manga, Yue implies that Yukito's feelings for Touya are mostly subconscious up until he realizes Touya sacrificed his magic for him — like Sakura, he didn't really realize he was falling in love with Touya until he already had. Unlike Sakura, the realization itself doesn't cause him any anguish, because he's instead got other realizations that are more pressing. He betrays a little wistfulness over his uncertainty that he isn't sure exactly how Touya feels about him, but Touya's affirmations end up being what helps him come to terms with the nature of his existence, and they are generally much less awkward and less worried about proving themselves to each other. Betty and Veronica: He's the Betty to Syaoran's Veronica towards Sakura's Archie because he is a warm, kind, friendly individual who also lives nearby. He gently rejects Sakura because of his feelings for T

characters.name: Meiling Li
characters.description: An anime-exclusive character and Syaoran's cousin and fiancée from Hong Kong. Meiling arrives in Tomoeda to help Syaoran capture the Clow Cards. At first aClingy Jealous Girland aBratty Half-Pint, Meiling eventually warms to Sakura and Tomoyo. Though she has no magical abilities despite being from the Li clan, she's still aBadass Normal, being a pro in martial arts alongside Syaoran. Action Girl: She's a pro in martial arts. Anguished Declaration of Love: She confessed her love to Syaoran when they were young, and she was tearing up and blushing madly while at it. Anime Chinese Girl: She comes from Hong Kong, wears Chinese dresses, sports odango pigtails and knows martial arts. Annoying Younger Sibling: She's Syaoran's cousin and self-proclaimed fiancée, but he treats her as his bratty and clingy sister figure more than anything.

characters.name: Eriol Hiiragizawa
characters.description: Eriol arrives as aNew Transfer Studentwho quickly befriends Sakura and Tomoyo. However he turns out to be an antagonist of sorts in the final third of the series, as he is really the reincarnation of Clow Reed. The Ace: He is skilled at more or less everything, apparently just like Clow Reed was. Adaptation Dye-Job: The parts of Eriol's signature mage costume that are red in the manga are blue in the anime. Badass Bookworm: He looks the quiet, studious type and he is also the most skilled mage alive. Benevolent Boss: He can be firm at times with Spinel and Ruby at times but he cares for them deeply, just as Clow Reed did Yue and Cerberus.

characters.name: Naoko Yanagisawa
characters.description: ACute Bookwormwho loves ghosts stories and is aNightmare Fetishist. All There in the Manual: HerLong-Distance Relationshipis only stated in an interview by the creators — apparently, it didn't make the final cut of the manga. Bespectacled Cutie: She's a cute and bookish girl with glasses. Curtains Match the Window: Brown hair and eyes. Cute Bookworm: She likes reading books and writing stories.

characters.name: Chiharu Mihara
characters.description: One of Sakura's close friends. She often exposes Yamazaki's lies to the bewildered Sakura and Syaoran. Adaptation Dye-Job: Her auburn hair from the manga was changed to chestnut brown in the anime. Berserk Button: She really, really,reallycannot tolerate Yamazaki's lies, leading her to (comically) strangle him whenever he starts telling them. Beta Couple: With Yamazaki because they have less drama than our alpha couple, Yamazaki's tendency to doobvious liesaside. Curtains Match the Window: Brown hair and eyes.

characters.name: Rika Sasaki
characters.description: A shy, nervous, but well-educated girl who has aPrecocious Crushon theCool TeacherMr. Terada. Adaptation Dye-Job: Rika has grayish hair in the manga, but the anime made it dark brown. Age-Gap Romance: She's an elementary school student while Mr. Terada is her thirty-something teacher. In the manga, they are a couple and are intending to get married when she comes of age. Brainwashed and Crazy: Briefly, when she puts on a sword-shaped pin that actually is the dormant form of The Sword card. Sakura debrainwashes her using The Illusion to confuse her, and then disarms her. Curtains Match the Window: Dark brown hair and eyes.

characters.name: Takashi Yamazaki
characters.description: Syaoran's best friend in school. Hiseyes are always closedand he has a humourous habit of making up exaggerated stories about the origins of various subjects, which Sakura and Syaoran always fall for. Bad Liar: No one believes his lies except forSakura and Syaoran,especiallyChiharu, who knows him like the palm of her hand andimmediatelystops himvery violentlywhen he goes overboard. Beta Couple: With Chiharu because they have less drama than our alpha couple. Butt-Monkey: Always on the receiving end of Chiharu's tantrums, which almost always come after he starts telling his lies. It's played far more seriously in the second movie, where he's seriously wounded during one of The Nothing's attacks. This allows Syaoran to take the role of the Prince in the school play — while Sakura is the Princess. Cross-Cast Role:In-Universe, he plays a female role in the school play that leads to Sakura capturing The Light and the Dark. His role changes depending on the media: in the manga he's the evil fairy, whereas in the animeMeilingbecomes the evil fairy and he plays the Queen Mother instead.Touya:[in the anime]... T-that Queen is played by a boy!?

characters.name: The Windy
characters.description: Blow You Away: Its primary magic is to create strong winds. Make Some Noise: Although the Windy card does not produce sounds, the sound-related cards (The Song and The Voice) are under its command. Our Fairies Are Different: The Windy's body vaguely resembles a fairy, long-haired and insect-winged. Pacifist Wind: As mentioned by Kero, she hates fighting and is used incapacitate rather inflicting harm. Starter Mon: The first Clow Card at Sakura's disposal and will remain as one of her most reliable cards.

characters.name: The Fly
characters.description: Adaptational Villainy: Fly was stated to be aggressive thanks to an injury on its leg in the manga; the anime turned it into a chaotic monster. Androcles' Lion: In the manga, Sakura is able to capture The Fly after comforting them from an injury. Giant Flyer: Its unsealed form is crane-like bird larger than houses. Power Gives You Wings: It gives Sakura wings to fly. Originally, it gave them to her staff, but after becoming a Sakura Card, the wings appear on Sakura's back instead. Sizeshifter: In the manga, Fly appears as giant due to it lashing out because of its injury. When it was comforted by Sakura, it shrunk to a smaller, infantile size.

characters.name: The Shadow
characters.description: Adaptational Villainy: Shadow was turned into a wicked prankster in the anime, while in the manga, it was explained that it was testing Sakura to see if she really was worthy of collecting the cards. Black Cloak: A non-villainous example. Shadow is nothing but a billowing,Grim Reaper-ish black cloak in its true form. Casting a Shadow: Shadow can control the shadows of people and objects at its master's command. It's also capable ofShadow Pinbecause it does all things magically. Living Shadow: Before being sealed, Shadow is a literal shadow that moves on its own. Logical Weakness: Shadow is captured by being exposed to very strong lights that cast no shadows.

characters.name: The Watery
characters.description: Beef Gate: Despite being situated in an aquarium close to Sakura's home, Kero attempts to discourage her from confronting the Watery, telling her that the cards she has on hand aren't enough to handle such an aggressive being. She still captures it by luring it into a freezer and locking it inside. Logical Weakness: Watery is captured by being lured into a freezer and locked inside. Making a Splash: Watery is able to control water and create whirlpools and tidal waves. Non-Humans Lack Attributes: When Watery is flying, she looks like a mermaid, she is naked, butshe has no nipples◊. Our Mermaids Are Different: The Watery appears as a mermaid with a fish tail.

characters.name: The Rain
characters.description: Flying on a Cloud: Rain rides on a cloud from which it releases rain. Happy Harlequin Hat: Rain appears as a little girl with a jester's hat that falls into two long points, tipped in teardrop shapes like rain drops. Making a Splash: It can generate and control rain.

characters.name: The Wood
characters.description: Elemental Hair Composition: Wood appears as a woman with leaves growing from her green hair. Garden Garment: Wood wears a dress made of leaves. Graceful Loser: In the anime, after Sakura captures the Rain, Wood reverts back to its normal form and simply turns itself back to card form, without Sakura needing to perform the locking, as she does to the other cards. Green Thumb: Wood has the power to manipulate and generate rampant vines, bushes, and branches. Non-Malicious Monster: Kero states that Wood is a gentle card by nature. It was only because of Rain's antics that they became a problem, and once Rain has been sealed, Wood immediately undoes all the damage and willfully returns to card form.

characters.name: The Jump
characters.description: Attack of the 50-Foot Whatever: Jump becomes jumbo-sized during its fight with Sakura. Bunnies for Cuteness: Its card depicts it as a cute bunny with angel wings. Hoist by His Own Petard: In the anime, Jump grows to immense size by forming a body with plushes and other toys and tries to stomp on Sakura, but loses its balance and falls to the ground, undoing its giant size form and allowing Sakura to capture it. In a Single Bound: Jump's magic grants Sakura the ability to leap to great heights. Killer Rabbit: It is shaped like a rabbit, and was pretty antagonistic when it first appeared, with a menacing look on its face.

characters.name: The Illusion
characters.description: A Glitch in the Matrix: In its episode, Sakura realizes "Nadeshiko" (her late mother)'s apparition is not her mother at all, and the false Nadeshiko begins to glitch out to show a kaleidoscopic image, revealing its true form. Master of Illusion: It has the power to create illusions, which take the form of what the observer wants or expects to see at the moment. Your Heart's Desire: In the "Sword" episode, Sakura uses the Illusion's powers to distract Rika (who is being manipulated by the Sword card) with her heart's desire: their teacher.

characters.name: The Silent
characters.description: Berserk Button: Silent hates noise so much that she teleports anyone who makes noise in front of her out of the room. Curse of Babel: The Silent presents a version of this, capable of stifling noise in a location. If the source proves too annoying, the Silent will teleport it away. Eyes Always Shut: Silent appears as a woman with her eyes closed. Teleportation: Silent can magically transport the source of any noise away from herself, albeit only over short distances.

characters.name: The Thunder
characters.description: Panthera Awesome: The Thunder appears as a lion/wolf-like beast made of electricity. Raijū: Thunder is clearly inspired by the RaijuYōkai, appearing as a wolf-like beast surrounded by lightning. Shock and Awe: Thunder has the power to summon large amounts of lightning and electricity to strike its enemy.

characters.name: The Sword
characters.description: Absurdly Sharp Blade: It's able to cut even through the otherwise unbreakable Shield, and even through Loop's magic. Cool Sword: Sword is a gorgeousRoyal Rapierwhen unsealed. Implausible Fencing Powers: The user seems to become an expert fencer while wielding it. Touch the Intangible: In the manga, Sakura's heart is strong enough that she can trap Shadow with The Sword card. Weapon Wields You: The Sword possesses its wielder and automatically makes them an expert fencer.

characters.name: The Flower
characters.description: Flowers of Femininity: The Flower card is depicted as a pretty young woman in a frilly, tiered gown and she has flower decorations that dangle from both of her ears. Forced Dance Partner: In its episode, Flower dances and twirls on the school rooftop. When she spots Sakura, she immediately takes the latter's hands and starts twirling around with her as well, until Sakura captures her. Genki Girl: All she wants to do is dance and create pretty flowers around her. When Sakura faces her, Flower takes her hands and happily tries to dance with her. Girlish Pigtails: She's the most feminine card and her hair is dressed in corkscrew pigtails. Graceful Loser: She offers no resistence when re-sealed, likely because she had fun and thinks it's okay to "go home" after she's done with it.

characters.name: The Shield
characters.description: Anti-Magic: In addition to protecting against physical attacks, it protects those within from the effects of magic, even things like time rewinding. Deflector Shields: Its power is to construct a powerful barrier. Special Ability Shield: The Shield card is a winged shield that creates a barrier around whatever its master wishes.

characters.name: The Time
characters.description: Father Time: The only Card that is unambiguously male, clearly inspired by the classic image of Father Time as a bearded old man. "Groundhog Day" Loop: The Time causes all of Tomoeda to repeat the same day. Only those with magical powers can sense the day being repeated. Supernatural Gold Eyes: Time's eyes are colored gold in the anime, to contrast with the brass colours of its robe, beard and body. Time Master: Time has free control over the movement of time, having the ability to speed it up, slow it down, freeze it, or even create a"Groundhog Day" Loop. Time Stands Still: Since Time requires a lot of power, Sakura and Syaoran are only able to use it to stop time for a short while.

characters.name: The Power
characters.description: Badass Adorable: Power appears as a cutesy-looking, pink-clad little girl, but no other card is as physically strong as her. Break the Haughty: She has a big ego in showing off her strength. After losing in tug-o-war, she loses her fighting spirit. Cute Bruiser: Resembles a little girl who packs a massive punch. Little Miss Badass: Power takes the form of a small girl and packs a massive punch. Pint-Sized Powerhouse: Despite her small size, Power is capable of lifting up an adult elephant.

characters.name: The Mist
characters.description: Super Smoke: The mist it creates can eat through almost any material, including metal bars and stone.

characters.name: The Storm
characters.description: Elemental Hair Colors: Storm's power is creating rainstorms and tornadoes, and appears as a child with curly gray hair. Weather Manipulation: Storm is capable of summoning huge rainstorms and tornadoes.

characters.name: The Float
characters.description: Not Quite Flight: It makes people float by manifesting as a hot-air balloon and letting them ride on it.

characters.name: The Erase
characters.description: Happy Harlequin Hat: It has a harlequin-esque design with a hat which ends in two points and has balls dangling from it. Power of the Void: Erase removes objects and people from existence. The effect is permanent if it's not undone within a certain time limit.

characters.name: The Glow
characters.description: Light 'em Up: Glow's power is creating tiny glowing lights. Showgirl Skirt: Glow appears as a little fairy-like girl wearing a leotard with two long coattails.

characters.name: The Move
characters.description: Awesome, but Impractical: On paper, The Move sounds like an extremely useful card, given that it has the ability to teleport things from one place to another. However, it can only move one object at a time, it can't move heavy objects, it can't move objects any bigger than a book or pencil, and it can only move non-living items. Given all these limitations, the card is essentially useless. Teleportation: Move is capable of inhabiting objects and magically moving them by making them disappear from one place and appear in another. However, Move can only move small inanimate objects and only for a short distance.

characters.name: The Fight
characters.description: Action Girl: The Fight takes the form of an Asian woman in combat attire similar to Meiling's, and is incredibly skilled in martial arts, so much that a lot of martial artists she challenged have (non-fatally) fallen victim to her. Anime Chinese Girl: She has a decidedly Chinese clothing andhairstyle, and she's associated with martial arts. Bare-Fisted Monk: She's the only card to fight bare handed. Cute Bruiser: She's the most straightforward punch-you-in-the-face card and has the humanoid form of an attractive young girl. Dull Surprise: Does not change her facade, even after Sakura clobbers her point blank using the Power card.

characters.name: The Loop
characters.description: Infinite: Its symbol looks like the infinity symbol. Space Master: It has the power to manipulate space and connect and splice different locations.

characters.name: The Sleep
characters.description: Astonishingly Appropriate Appearance: The Sleep card has a wing growing out of its ear. Some statues of Greek deity Hypnos, the god of sleep, depict him either with a pair of wings on its head, or with a wing for an ear. Cosmic Motifs: Sleep has a star on its leotard and on its head, and it wields a wand with a moon tip. Ear Wings: Sleep has one, overlarge, feathered ear on the left side of the head. Fairy Sexy: Sleep appears as a bluish-white pixie wearing a periwinkle bodysuit. Forced Sleep: Sleep induces sleep to people, although those with high magic power can resist the effect.

characters.name: The Song
characters.description: Astonishingly Appropriate Appearance: Apropos for a music-based card, she has a treble-shaped ear ornament and her dress resembles a string instrument. The Bait: Sakura has Song imitate Tomoyo's singing again to lure The Voice card out of hiding. Voice Changeling: She perfectly copies Tomoyo's singing voice.

characters.name: The Little
characters.description: Cute Oversized Sleeves: Little uses oversized sleeves to symbolize her cuteness. Hair Antennae: She has a pair of antennae sticking out of her hair. Happy Harlequin Hat: She wears a jester's hat. Incredible Shrinking Man: Little can make her targets small like a mouse. It Amused Me: She shrinks Sakura for her own amusement, regardless of how she feels.

characters.name: The Mirror
characters.description: Attack Reflector: She can manifest as an actual mirror to reflect attacks. Confronting Your Imposter: Naturally, shows up Sakura confront the imposter. Criminal Doppelgänger: Since she was aware of who is collecting the cards, Mirror decided to damage Sakura's reputation as a form of taking her out. She did this by going around causing disturbances in local stores. Cute Oversized Sleeves: Mirror's true form appears as a cute girl wearing a Chinese style dress with long sleeves that covers her hands. Dead Person Impersonation: Toya is uncomfortable with Mirror using Sakura's form because it makes it look like Sakura is dead.

characters.name: The Maze
characters.description: Cheaters Never Prosper: Sakura cannot use The Fly to find the exit, nor use The Sword to slice through the walls. Although Ms.Mizuki is able break through it with the Moon Bell. The Maze: As might be expected, The Maze card takes the form of an inescapable maze. Mobile Maze: In its episode, Sakura tries to use The Fly card on her staff to fly over the maze, but it extends its green walls to stop her escape attempt. When Sakura and her friends exit the maze, Sakura is advised to seal it at once, for it is rearranging itself.

characters.name: The Return
characters.description: Intangible Time Travel: Allows one to travel back to the past but not change it. Mages are also able to detect the presence of the otherwise invisible time travelers.

characters.name: The Shot
characters.description: Anime Hair: Shot has very long, spiky red hair that resembles a mane. Energy Weapon: Shot can fire laser beams at targets.

characters.name: The Sweet
characters.description: Anime Hair: She has afro-like hair resembling cotton candy. Food-Based Superpowers: Sweet is able to turn objects into edible sweets or make foods taste sweeter. Girlish Pigtails: She has a pair of poofy low pigtails. Sweet Tooth: Sweet's weakness is sweet things, because when she finds one she can't help but approach it to make it even sweeter.

characters.name: The Dash
characters.description: All Animals Are Dogs: The Dash's physical form is a small fox-like creature and is perky and friendly towards humans. It even growls when agitated or licks people when affectionate. Both Sakura and Rei even refer to it as a "good boy". Beware the Nice Ones: Dash was among the few cards that didn't antagonise Sakura after it was released, befriending a young girl and acting as her pet. When Sakura haphazardously tried to turn it into a Sakura Card however, it went berserk, growling at Sakura and running rampant until she managed to calm it down. Carbuncle Creature: The Dash takes the form of a fox/cat/rabbit hybrid with a blue jewel on its forehead. Ridiculously Cute Critter: Dash is an adorably cute fox-like creature. Super-Speed: Dash is able to run at supernatural speeds for short distances. It can grant this ability to others as well.

characters.name: The Big
characters.description: Attack of the 50-Foot Whatever: Big can make her targets grow to the size of a tower. Gentle Giant: Big is shown as a very tall young woman who is also very kind and gentle, aside of enlarging objects and living beings. When she's sealed away, she complies without any protests. Happy Harlequin Hat: She wears a jester's hat similar to The Little's. Statuesque Stunner: Big's unsealed form is that of a beautiful, tall and quiet woman in a fairy outfit.

characters.name: The Create
characters.description: Behemoth Battle: Sakura uses the Big to match The Create's dragon in size. Reality-Writing Book: Create brings the images from its writer’s head to life. Rewriting Reality: Create, appearing as an unfinished storybook, allows the writer to create whatever they jot down in its pages with its quill. However, all that is created vanishes when they stop writing. Wingding Eyes: Every creature Naoko create has swirly eyes, including the huge dragon.

characters.name: The Change
characters.description: Cartoon Creature: Its form resembles a lizard or chameleon. "Freaky Friday" Flip: Change can make two people swap bodies, as it does to Syaoran and Kero.

characters.name: The Freeze
characters.description: Harmless Freezing: People freezed by it can be thawed with no side effects. Ice Magic Is Water: Freeze is aligned under The Watery card. An Ice Person: It's able to freeze almost anything, emit ice as a weapon, and lower the temperature of a localized area to freezing point (and possibly beyond it).

characters.name: The Firey
characters.description: Elemental Rivalry: In the manga, Firey looksverypleased to duel with Watery after Sakura summons her. Fire Is Masculine: Firey is the only elemental card to look more like a boy than a girl. Flaming Hair: Firey's hair is permanently set on fire. My Nayme Is:"Firey", not "Fiery". Playing with Fire: Firey can create powerful fires from thin air, as well as manipulate any existing flames.

characters.name: The Arrow
characters.description: Girlish Pigtails: She appears as a young girl with a pair of long pigtails held by balls at the sides of her head. Long-Range Fighter: Being an archer, her fighting style consists of firing arrows from the distance. Rain of Arrows: She can summon a volley of arrows and fire them at her opponent. Showgirl Skirt: She wears a dress that fans out in the shape of two fins at the back and reveals her shorts.

characters.name: The Bubbles
characters.description: Mundane Utility: Said to have been created by Clow in order to bathe Kerberos. Offscreen Moment of Awesome: Despite its capture not being depicted in the anime, in the firstMidquelfilm, Tomoyo and Sakura watch a recording of its capture,caughton tape. Our Mermaids Are Different: Bubbles' card is a cute mermaid with a dolphin tail. Remember the New Guy?: Caught off-screen between episodes 35 and 36. Water Is Womanly: The Bubbles is under The Watery and has a similar card design that depicts her as a mermaid.

characters.name: The Through
characters.description: Awesome, but Impractical: The Through allows the user to pass through walls, but if they're too thick, then the user runs the risk of being stuck forever. Kero dismisses its use when trying to find Tomoyo during one of Eriol's trials when Syaoran suggested it. Intangibility: Through allows its user to pass through solid objects, though it's risky depending on the user's magic power and the thickness of the object to be passed through. Remember the New Guy?: Caught off-screen between episodes 35 and 36. Although it was mentioned a few times, it was never actually used on-screen.

characters.name: The Wave
characters.description: Making a Splash: Its powers were never shown, but it can be assumed it controls waves or tides. Remember the New Guy?: Caught off-screen between episodes 35 and 36. It was never used or even alluded to.

characters.name: The Libra
characters.description: Lie Detector: Can determine if a person is lying or telling the truth. Remember the New Guy?: Caught off-screen between episodes 35 and 36. It was never used or even alluded to.

characters.name: The Snow
characters.description: Hime Cut: She appears as a woman wearing a kimono and having blunt bangs, cheek-length sidelocks, and long hair. Ice Magic Is Water: Snow is aligned under The Watery. An Ice Person: Snow has the ability to create and control the snow around her. Yuki-onna: Snow's design is based on a yuki-onna, right down to the white kimono.

characters.name: The Voice
characters.description: Ear Wings: She has wing-like ears. Harping on About Harpies: She looks like a large pink bird with the head of a cute little girl. Intangible Theft: She steals Tomoyo's voice, rendering her mute until Sakura captures the card. Winged Humanoid: She has wings for arms.

characters.name: The Lock
characters.description: It Was with You All Along: Sakura's key is what unlocks The Lock, allowing it to be captured. Supernatural Sealing: It has the power to magically lock anything and preventing their unlocking by conventional means. For example, Lock can seal off rooms by magically shielding any and all exits such that they cannot be penetrated or destroyed.

characters.name: The Cloud
characters.description: Cumulonemesis: The Cloud is depicted as a small child with puffy hair that resembles clouds, and is able to create and manipulate clouds. While not necessarily a 'nemesis', Sakura and Syaoran have to capture it and transform it back into a card as part of their mission (and the anime's premise). Delirious Misidentification: A minor example. In its episode, Sakura was with a fever, but still had to capture the card. When it returns to a more manageable form, Sakura mistakes it for the Rain card. Elemental Hair Composition: Cloud's hair is poofy and cloud-like, with straight bangs on either side of its face ending in cloud puffs. Purple Is Powerful: In the anime, the manifestation of its powers is a huge dark purple cloud. After Syaoran summons a fire to evaporate most of the clouds, it shows its true form: a light purple child-like body. Weather Manipulation: Cloud creates thick clouds all over the sky which it can move however it pleases.

characters.name: The Dream
characters.description: Dreaming of Things to Come: Dream provides prophetic visions for Sakura, showing her the future to come in her sleep. Eye-Obscuring Hat: Dream wears a tall headdress that obscures her eyes. Your Heart's Desire: Dream is able to make people dream their greatest desires, like making Tomoyo see a parade of Sakuras wearing the costumes she designed.

characters.name: The Sand
characters.description: Bedlah Babe: Sand's card depicts a woman dressed in Arabic clothes (harem pants, crop top, sleeves and shoes). Moment Killer: Starts causing chaos right when Sakura is practicing thekiss scenewith Syaoran for the Sleeping Beauty play. Sand Blaster: It has the ability to manipulate sand.

characters.name: The Light and The Dark
characters.description: Anti-Magic: The Dark prevents Sakura from using any form of magic. Cool Crown: Both wear a spiked crown. Cosmic Motifs: The Light's card image depicts her holding a cloud and the sun in her hands, and she also has a sun emblazoned on her upper chest. The Dark's card has it holding a six-pointed star and a moon in her hands. Cynic–Idealist Duo: A mild example. Light believed in Sakura's power from the start, and chose tohide in her heartfor that very reason, as she confidently said to Dark. Meanwhile, Dark really needed to see Sakura in action in order to accept her as the Cardcaptor, and rather than encouraging her like Light did, she simply told her to do her best, as Yue would be the one to decide after the Final Judgement. Dark Is Not Evil: The Dark in fact loves Sakura along with the other Clow Cards.

characters.name: The Twin
characters.description: Cute Oversized Sleeves: They look like cute small children with oversized sleeves that could touch the floor. Happy Harlequin Hat: They wear double-pointed jester hats. Single-Minded Twins: Both twins form a single card, look identical and move in perfect sync. Trickster Twins: They're a pair of mischievous twins.

characters.name: The Earthy
characters.description: Astonishingly Appropriate Appearance: The Earthy card depicts a woman wearing a crystal-like bodice, and decorated with mineral-like adornments. A mineral structure also protrudes from the woman's chest. All serve to visually indicate its earth-related powers. Dishing Out Dirt:Earthy is able to freely control and manipulate the element of earth and its various aspects; such as soil, rock, and stone.The Sand, despite being an independent card/element, is also placed under The Earthy's command/aligment. Earthy is able to freely control and manipulate the element of earth and its various aspects; such as soil, rock, and stone. The Sand, despite being an independent card/element, is also placed under The Earthy's command/aligment. Pre-Final Boss: Being the final Clow Card Sakura collects before facing the Final Judgement.

characters.name: The Nameless Card
characters.description: Dub Name Change: Becomes the Hope card in the Nelvana dub. Never Given a Name: The card is nameless. The Power of Love: This card was created from Sakura's love for Syaoran. InThe Sealed Card, it's the card's intervention what prevents Syaoran loses his love for Sakura when The Nothing card is sealed. Uniformity Exception: This card stands out as the only card that started out as a Sakura card as an original creation by Sakura rather than a Clow card changing ownership.

characters.name: The Nothing
characters.description: The Nothing is introduced in the second filmThe Sealed Card. Created by Clow Reed as a negative counterbalance to the positive magic of the other cards, the Nothing was kept separated from the others beneath Clow's mansion. She is freed after the mansion is demolished, and begins stealing the cards to reunite with her friends. Amusement Park of Doom: Her hideout, the new amusement park built where Clow's mansion was, becomes quite scary when things start vanishing. Anti-Villain: The Woobie variation. Despite all the destruction she causes, it's very hard not to sympathize with her because her own creator sealed her in a dark place for centuries and she antagonizes Sakura because she wants to take the other Cards from her so she isn't alone anymore. Being Evil Sucks: Her rampage only leads the other cards to reject her. Instead they and Sakura offer her aHeel–Face Turn. Dark Is Evil:Downplayed. She acts as the negative counterbalance to the positive magic of the Clow Cards and even though her actions are destructive, she's just lonely and sad instead of evil.

characters.name: The Hope
characters.description: Fusion Dance: The Hope is created when The Nameless Card fuses with The Nothing.

characters.name: Clow Reed
characters.description: Creator of the Clow Cards, Kero and Yue, Clow Reed died before the manga and anime. A powerful sorcerer in his own right, he is reincarnated as Eriol. Adaptation Dye-Job: His hair's supposed to be black/dark gray, but is made dark blue in the anime. The Archmage: Stated to be the most powerful sorcerer of all time as a result of being born from the union of two powerful magical families, one from England and the other from China. A deconstructed example, as his powers were so great that he couldn't even do magic in small scales, and he accidentally unbalanced the entire universe. Due to this, he decided to create the Clow Cards (which have a set power to their effects) with the intent of someone inheriting his magic and returning balance to the universe. Blessed with Suck: Incredibly, phenomenally powerful magic... which led him to be constantly aware of the future. (And that's just in CCS. Check×××HOLiCfor what he does by accident). According to Eriol in the manga, the reason he split his reincarnation in two was because he no longer wanted to be the most powerful magician in the world anymore. But Not Too Foreign: Half-British and half-Chinese. This influenced the way he developed his magic, since he combined elements of Eastern and Western magic to create the Cards.

characters.name: Yue
characters.description: The second guardian of the Clow Cards assigned to appoint the next owner of the cards. He becomes an ally to Sakura after she passes the Final Judgement. Adaptation Dye-Job: Yue's uniform is entirely made up of shades of blue and white in the anime, but in the original manga it also had some yellow, and the gems on his wrists and feet were amber. Bodyguard Crush: Yue's role is that of a guardian, and it's heavily implied that Yue had feelings for Clow Reed which plays a part in why Yue has such a difficult time letting go and moving on to a new master. Can't Live Without You: As Yue represents the moon, his powers mimic the way the moon reacts to light. The moon cannot make its own light, and is dependent on the light of the sun to glow, just as Yue is dependent on his Master's power for strength. Unfortunately, Sakura is weaker than Clow Reed at the time, so her magic is unable to sustain Yue's power and existence. The Comically Serious: His serious demeanour sometimes contributes to the comedy, such as Episode 58. Also, why does he agree to Tomoyo staging a dynamic fight scene in the opening to the send movie?

characters.name: Nakuru Akizuki, aka Ruby Moon
characters.description: One of Eriol's two minions, if you want to call them that. A hyperactive but manipulativeGenki Girl, as Nakuru she has apparent affections for Toya. In her true form Ruby Moon she can fly and has similar powers to Yue. The Ace: Like Yukito, Ruby isalsoridiculously good at sports andalsorefuses to join any team. Must be a magical-guardian-affiliated-with-the-moon trait. Adaptation Dye-Job: Nakuru's hair goes from light auburn in the manga to dark brown in the anime. Likewise, Ruby Moon's color scheme changes from red and black to pink and indigo. Ambiguous Gender Identity: Nakuru presents "herself" as female because girl's clothes are cuter, but she's technically genderless and she also admits gender identity doesn't really matter to her, so she could be considered gender fluid.Nakuru: "I look good in a skirt! (...) Well, it's not like gender really matters... I'm not a human being anyway..." Berserk Button: She doesn't like it when Spinel calls her a cross-dresser.

characters.name: Spinel Sun
characters.description: The second of Eriol's minions, and a counterpart to Kero. EssentiallyThe Eeyore, Spinel is quiet and calm but can go hyperactive when he eats sweets. His true form is a butterfly-winged panther who has powers similar to Keroberos. Accidental Misnaming: Nakuru repeatedly calls him "Suppi," much to his annoyance."Who are you calling Suppi?!" Adaptational Comic Relief: His interactions with Kero and his allergy to sweets are exclusive to the anime. In the manga, he never interacts with Kero and his personality is simply a more serious foil to the latter. Berserk Button: Sugar, though not berserk in anangryway, quite the opposite. Cats Are Snarky: Lots of clever remarks in his smaller form.

characters.name: Kaho Mizuki
characters.description: A mysterious butCool Teacherwho starts teaching at Sakura's school, but is actually a powerful magician of some kind. Back for the Finale: She spends most of the final arc part away in England, but comes back at the end. Beauty Equals Goodness: A beautiful woman who has only ever been helpful to Sakura, despite Syaoran's insiting that she might be up to no good. Catchphrase: "Just a hunch". Character Exaggeration: In the manga, despite being presented as a mysterious, mature and nice lady whose presence flusters Sakura and makes Syaoran suspicious, she can also be quite clumsy, cheerful and as Eriol and themanuals pointed out, forgets about streets and tends to get lost a lot of times. The anime puts more emphasis on her mature and mysterious facade instead, probably totrick the audienceinto believing she's an enemy.

characters.name: The Enchantress (Madoushi / Su Yung)
characters.description: TheBig Badof the first movie. An extremely powerful Chinese sorceress, the Enchantress (also called Madoushi or Su Yung in the dub) used to be a fortune teller in Hong Kong until her business was destroyed by Clow Reed's arrival, making her despise him deeply and preventing her spirit from moving on after her death. In the English dub, she was Clow Reed's lover for a time but when she began abusing her magic, they battled, forcing Clow to imprison her within another dimension. Centuries on, she seeks freedom and lures Sakura to Hong Kong, mistaking her magical aura for Clow's. Big Bad: She is the villain in the first movie. Dark Magical Girl: The contrast with Sakura, theMagical Girl, is revealed over the course of the movie. She was once a good-heartened fortune teller, and turned sour after Clow indirectly drove her business to ruins. As stated in the trope above, after fighting theMagical Girland losing, she has aHeel–Face Turnand passes on in peace. Evil Is Hammy:Nicole Oliverdoes a great job of showing the Enchantress' rage (e.g. "I will have my revenge!") Evil Is Petty: Only in the original Japanese, where she wanted revenge on Clow Reed for unintentionally wrecking her fortune telling business. Her motivation in the English dub is slightly more understandable.

characters.name: Sonomi Daidouji
characters.description: Neé Amamiya. She is Tomoyo's mother and Nadeshiko's best friend and cousin. She resents Fujitaka from marrying Nadeshiko at such a young age but is thrilled to be in Sakura's company. Always Second Best: Her feelings for Nadeshiko have led to a fierce if one sided rivalry with Fujitaka. Unfortunately for her she's never been able to defeat Fujitaka in any kind of contest, barring one moment in the second movie. Ambiguously Bi: She was in love with Nadeshiko, although ultimately married a man surnamed Daidouji and nothing indicates she doesn't have a happy marriage. However, this is pure speculation, as the circumstances surrounding her husband have never been explained; it's even unclear whether she is still married at this point. Big Fancy House: Her house is about as big as a park. Childhood Friend Romance: She was in love with Nadeshiko since Kindergarten.

characters.name: Yoshiyuki Terada
characters.description: The on-and-off teacher of Sakura's class. Rika has a crush on him which he seems to acknowledge to different degrees depending in the media. Adaptational Romance Downgrade: In the manga, not only is Terada fully aware of Rika's romantic affections for him, he reciprocates and has promised to marry her when she's of age. Due to the creepy factor of the majorAge-Gap Romancebetween an adult teacher and his elementary school student, the anime heavily downplays the relationship and turns it into a one-sidedPrecocious Crushon Rika's part, while Terada is madeOblivious to Love. Age-Gap Romance: He's a man in his thirties while Rika is his elementary school student. In the manga, he asks her to marry him when she comes of age and she accepts. The Jailbait Wait: In the manga, he likes Rika back, but he's waiting until she's legal to marry her. Satellite Love Interest: There's nothing noteworthy about him other than being the handsome teacher Sakura's friend Rika has a crush on. Even more so in the manga where all his appearances in which he isn't working as a teacher are about his mutualTeacher/Student Romancewith Rika.

characters.name: Maki Matsumoto
characters.description: The owner of the Twin Bells shop in Tomoeda, Maki has trouble due to the troublesome Jump Card, but is helped out by Sakura and her friends. Alliterative Name:MakiMatsumoto Canon Foreigner: She's an anime-only character. Dub Name Change: In the English dub, she becomes Maggie. Friendly Shopkeeper: She's the kind and gentle owner of a toy store that sells stuffed toys and accessories. Sakura and her friends sometimes visit her store and she treats them well.

characters.name: Masaki Amamiya
characters.description: Sonomi and Nadeshiko's grandfather and Sakura's great-grandfather, Masaki cherished his two granddaughters and heavily objected to Nadeshiko marrying Fujitaka. After her death, Masaki did not see his great-grand children, until Sakura meets him. The Atoner: Masaki regrets his rejection of Nadeshiko and Fujitaka's marriage, and decides to properly welcome Fujitaka. Big Fancy House: AmassiveEuropean-style mansion with luxurious gardens. Boyfriend-Blocking Dad: He was very objective to his granddaughter Nadeshiko marrying Fujitaka when she was still a teenager and ultimately cut contact with her over it. He's remorseful about this in present day. Cool Old Guy: He's a rather swell and soft-spoken old man, and easily bonds with Sakura.

characters.name: Li Yelan
characters.description: Syaoran's strict, stern mother and head of the Li family. She is an incredibly powerful sorceress. Almighty Mom: Yesh. Syaoran is really scared of her, and her very energetic teen daughters all settle down (even if not completely) when she's around. Aloof Dark-Haired Girl: HasRaven Hair, Ivory Skin, and is the cold, perfectionist matriarch of the Li clan admired by her own daughters. The Beautiful Elite: An elegant and sophisticated head of her clan. Big Fancy House: The Li main house is a classical Chinese family home. It is especially impressive considering that the Lis are from theinfamouslycrammedHong Kong.

characters.name: Li Xiehua, Li Huenli, Li Fuurie and Li Feimei
characters.description: Syaoran's eccentric four sisters. They are easily attracted to cute people. Ascended Extra: Syaoran mentions them and their mother in the manga and anime, but they're only seen in the first movie. Author Avatar: Said to be stand-ins forCLAMPthemselves. Color-Coded Characters: They can be distinguished by their haistyles and the colors of their dresses. (In case someoneus wondering, from left to right: Fuurie (or sometimes Fuutie) wears yellow and her long hair reaches her hips, Xiehua (Siefa) wearsdark pinkand has a bob, Huenli (Fanren) wears green and has short hair plus a long tail, and Feimei has shoulder-length hair while wearingblue. Cool Big Sis: The four girls are pretty sweet and cute, and are super thrilled to meet Sakura and the rest of the cast.

characters.name: Wei Wang
characters.description: Syaoran and Meiling'sParental Substitutewhilst they live in Tomoeda. He seems to have a degree of magical powers, and isThe Jeevesfor Syaoran. Canon Foreigner: He only appears in the anime (Syaoran lives in Japan alone in the manga). Hired Help as Family: He's officially Syaoran's butler, but both Syaoran and Meiling think of him as family and their father figure. The Jeeves: He is Syaoran's butler and inexplicably British. Morality Pet: Even in their earlier arrogant phases,Syaoran and Meilingare both respectful towards him.

characters.name: Yuuki Tachibana
characters.description: A youngtomboywho meets Sakura and Tomoyo during their attempt to capture the Silent. Bifauxnen: Both Sakura and Tomoyo believe her to be a boy untilher cap falls off and releases her long hairat the end of the episode. Compressed Hair: She has waist-length hair that she can hide under a cap. Curtains Match the Window: Brown hair and eyes. Daddy's Girl: Throughout the episode, her goal is to fix a painting that her Dad drew and the Silent messed up by hiding in. she's very proud of her father's work.

characters.name: Rei Tachibana
characters.description: The localPassionate Sports Girl, a very talented runner who is also Sakura and her friends' sempai. Bifauxnen: Pre-teen version, thanks to herBoyish Short Hairand her boyish civilian clothes. Boyish Short Hair: She has short hair to highlight her athletic character. Brought Down to Badass: She didn't need Dash's help to win a track meet after all. Friend to All Living Things: She takes The Dash in thinking it's a lost cat, and treats it very kindly.

characters.name: Youko Nakagawa
characters.description: One of Touya and Yukito's highschool classmates, she has a huge crush on Touya. She is a member of the drama club, and sees the upcoming school play as the chance to tell him that she loves him. Then, the Mist Card starts messing around. Bifauxnen: She's got short hair and can play male roles pretty well. Cross-Cast Role: She plays the Prince in theSchool Playfor "Cinderella". Destination Defenestration: Subverted, she was about to fall off a balcony — but said balcony was a part of the scenario, not an actual one. Distress Ball: Poor Youko justhadto be on stage with Touya right as the Mist starts dissolving and corrupting the stage itself...

characters.name: Akane
characters.description: A cute little girl who takes Kero in (sorta) after he runs away from Sakura's home. Disappeared Dad: Her father died a few years ago and shedoesn't really understand the implications, still kinda believing he's gone far away. It backfires when Akane finds The Float and it begins to lift her up to the skies, trying to fulfill her wish to see him again. Wise Beyond Their Years: She can handle housework pretty well at kindergarten age, since her mother has to work long hours to support the two.
